The liver is an important organ in the body, but it does not directly belong to the muscular system. However, it plays a crucial role in the [digestive] process by breaking down [carbohydrates], [proteins], and [fats] into smaller molecules that the body can use. The liver also helps in the [production] of [albumin], which is essential for maintaining [blood pressure] and [fluid balance]. It stores [vitamin] [D], [B12], and [K], which are vital for [bone health] and [blood clotting]. The liver works in conjunction with the [digestive] system to ensure that nutrients are properly [absorbed] and [distributed] throughout the body. It also helps in the [metabolism] of [cholesterol] and [glycogen], which are important for energy storage and release. The liver supports the [immune] system by producing [white blood cells] and [antibodies] that help fight [infections]. Finally, the liver plays a role in the [excretion] of [toxins] and [waste products] from the body.
